title: Rewrite Capture and CaptureResult IPC message types
description:
Rewrite the Capture and CaptureResult structs in crates/wtd-ipc/src/message.rs.

NEW Capture struct (all new fields are optional with serde defaults):
  target: String                    — required, pane path
  lines: Option<u32>               — last N lines (scrollback + visible, counted from bottom)
  all: Option<bool>                — return entire buffer (all scrollback + visible)
  after: Option<String>            — exact substring anchor (search newest-first in scrollback, capture from match line to end)
  after_regex: Option<String>      — regex anchor (same search behavior)
  max_lines: Option<u32>           — cap total lines returned (trims oldest if exceeded)
  count: Option<bool>              — return metadata only (line count, anchor status), no text

Precedence: (1) anchor found → from anchor to end, (2) anchor not found → fall back to lines/all/visible, (3) lines → last N, (4) all → entire buffer, (5) nothing → visible screen only. max_lines always caps. count suppresses text.

NEW CaptureResult struct:
  text: String                      — captured text (empty if count mode)
  lines: u32                        — number of lines in text (or would-be count)
  total_lines: u32                  — total lines available in buffer (scrollback + visible)
  anchor_found: Option<bool>        — whether anchor was found (only set when anchor requested)
  cursor: Option<u32>               — absolute line index where capture started (0 = oldest scrollback)

Keep the existing Scrollback/ScrollbackResult types untouched.

Update all serde round-trip tests in the same file. Ensure {"target":"x"} still deserializes (optional fields default to None). Update the TypedMessage enum and dispatch table if needed.

Completion evidence: cargo test -p wtd-ipc passes. Old minimal JSON deserializes correctly.
close_reason: Completed: rewrote Capture (6 optional fields) and CaptureResult (lines, total_lines, anchor_found, cursor) in wtd-ipc/src/message.rs; expanded roundtrip tests to 67; cargo test -p wtd-ipc passes

title: Implement capture_extended() on ScreenBuffer
description:
Add a capture_extended() method to ScreenBuffer in crates/wtd-pty/src/screen.rs.

Signature: pub fn capture_extended(&self, lines: Option<u32>, all: bool, after: Option<&str>, after_regex: Option<&regex::Regex>, max_lines: Option<u32>, count_only: bool) -> CaptureExtendedResult

Where CaptureExtendedResult is a local struct:
  text: String        — captured text (empty if count_only)
  lines: u32          — line count
  total_lines: u32    — scrollback.len() + self.rows
  anchor_found: Option<bool>
  cursor: u32         — absolute line index of capture start

Mental model: the buffer is a continuous sequence of lines — scrollback[0] (oldest) through scrollback[N-1] (newest), then visible row 0 through visible row R-1. Total = scrollback.len() + self.rows.

Algorithm:
1. Compute total_lines = scrollback.len() + self.rows
2. If after/after_regex set: search scrollback newest-first (index total-1 down to 0, checking scrollback rows then visible rows). On match, set start_line = match index. If not found, set anchor_found=false and fall through to lines/all/visible.
3. If lines set (and no anchor found): start_line = total_lines - lines
4. If all set: start_line = 0
5. If nothing: start_line = scrollback.len() (visible only)
6. Apply max_lines: if (total_lines - start_line) > max_lines, adjust start_line forward
7. Build text: iterate from start_line to total_lines, converting each line (scrollback row or visible row) to text using the cells_to_string pattern (skip wide_continuation, trim trailing spaces for scrollback rows, keep full width for visible rows)
8. If count_only: set text to empty, still compute line count

Add regex = { workspace = true } to crates/wtd-pty/Cargo.toml (already added to workspace Cargo.toml).

Add a helper fn cells_to_string(cells: &[Cell]) -> String that extracts text from a cell slice.

Unit tests (in the same file or a test module):
- Default (no options) → returns visible screen only
- lines=5 → returns last 5 lines from buffer
- all=true → returns entire buffer
- after='MARKER' found → returns from marker line to end
- after='MISSING' not found, lines=10 → falls back to last 10 lines
- after_regex matching → correct start line
- max_lines caps output
- count_only → empty text, correct line count
- cursor value is correct in each case

Completion evidence: cargo test -p wtd-pty passes with all new tests.
close_reason: Completed: Added capture_extended() to ScreenBuffer with CaptureExtendedResult, cells_to_string helper, anchor search (after/after_regex), lines/all/max_lines/count_only options, and 9 unit tests. All 49 wtd-pty tests pass.

1. REQUEST HANDLER (crates/wtd-host/src/request_handler.rs):

       target: String,
       #[arg(long)] lines: Option<u32>,
       #[arg(long)] all: bool,
       #[arg(long, value_name = "STRING")] after: Option<String>,
       #[arg(long, value_name = "PATTERN")] after_regex: Option<String>,
       #[arg(long)] max_lines: Option<u32>,
       #[arg(long)] count: bool,
     }

3. CLI DISPATCH (crates/wtd-cli/src/dispatch.rs):

   - Text mode, count: print result.lines as a number to stdout

   Update the format_text dispatch to handle the new CaptureResult shape (lines field is now u32, not absent).

5. Fix any compile errors from the CaptureResult field changes in existing tests/code.

Completion evidence: cargo build --workspace compiles. cargo test --workspace passes.

title: Fix UI attach so focused panes accept keyboard input
description:
Opening `wtd-ui --workspace <name>` against a live workspace can show only a small grey rectangle in the top-left of the pane and accept no keyboard input.

Observed behavior:
- `wtd open shell` succeeds.
- `wtd-ui --workspace shell` opens a window.
- The pane does not show the attached session contents.
- Typing does nothing.

Likely root cause:
- On `HostEvent::Connected`, the UI marks itself connected but does not hydrate the layout tree or `pane_sessions` mapping from the attach snapshot.
- Keyboard forwarding in `crates/wtd-ui/src/main.rs` depends on `pane_sessions.get(&focused)`, so an empty map drops all input.

Scope:
- Parse the attach snapshot from `AttachWorkspaceResult.state`.
- Rebuild the connected-mode `LayoutTree` from the host snapshot.
- Populate pane-to-session bindings from `paneStates` so focused panes can forward keyboard input.
- Add a regression test that proves attach-to-live-workspace enables typing into the focused pane.

Success criteria:
- After `wtd-ui --workspace shell`, typing in the focused pane reaches the attached session.
- The focused pane path/session state are consistent with the attached workspace snapshot.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix repo-local workspace discovery using client cwd
description:
`wtd open <name>` and `wtd list workspaces` do not discover repo-local `.wtd/<name>.yaml` files when the host process was started from a different working directory.

Repro:
1. In `C:\Work\WinTermDriver`, create `.wtd\shell.yaml`.
2. Run `./target/debug/wtd.exe open shell`.
3. Observe: `workspace 'shell' not found: workspace definition not found: shell`.
4. Run `./target/debug/wtd.exe list workspaces --json`.
5. Observe: empty list, even though `.wtd\shell.yaml` exists.
6. Run `./target/debug/wtd.exe open shell --file ./.wtd/shell.yaml`.
7. Observe: succeeds.

Likely root cause:
- Host-side request handling resolves discovery with `std::env::current_dir()` inside the host process rather than the caller's cwd.
- This affects both `load_workspace_from_disk` and `list_workspaces`.

Impact:
- Breaks the documented local `.wtd/` workflow unless users always pass `--file`.
- Makes `wtd list workspaces` misleading for project-local workspaces.

Expected:
- Repo-local workspace discovery should use the client invocation cwd, or an explicit cwd passed through the request.

Workaround:
- Use `wtd open <name> --file ./.wtd/<name>.yaml`.
close_reason: Completed

title: Preserve modified Enter semantics for live keyboard input
description:
Investigation wintermdriver-u1x found that WTD collapses Shift+Enter to the same bytes as plain Enter.

Root cause:
- crates/wtd-ui/src/input.rs key_event_to_bytes() maps KeyName::Enter to 0x0D regardless of modifiers
- crates/wtd-host/src/terminal_input.rs key-spec encoding also maps Enter to 0x0D regardless of modifiers
- Upstream Codex enables keyboard enhancement flags specifically so modified Enter is distinguishable from plain Enter and only advertises Shift+Enter newline behavior when enhanced keys are supported

Expected direction:
- preserve or synthesize distinct modified-Enter encoding for live keyboard input
- align WTD behavior with terminals that support keyboard enhancement / CSI-u style disambiguation
- ensure both live UI keyboard handling and injected key-spec handling are consistent
close_reason: Implemented modified Enter CSI-u encoding in UI and host paths

title: Seed initial pane rendering when UI attaches to live workspace
description:
Even after the UI attaches to a live workspace, the pane can appear visually empty until fresh output arrives from the host.

Observed behavior:

- A pane may render as an empty placeholder / small grey rectangle instead of showing the current terminal contents immediately.

Scope:
- Extend the attach path so the UI paints meaningful pane content immediately after attach instead of waiting for subsequent `SessionOutput` events.

Success criteria:
- Attaching the UI to an already-running workspace shows current pane contents immediately or via a deterministic initial sync step.
- The pane no longer appears blank until new output happens.

Start wtd-host, open a workspace from a YAML file via 'wtd open', send input via 'wtd send', capture output via 'wtd capture', and verify the captured output matches. Verify 'wtd list', 'wtd inspect', and 'wtd close' all return correct results. This is the gate test for Slice 5 headless integration. Expected outcome: all wtd CLI commands work against a real host process. Completion evidence: test script exercises open/send/capture/list/inspect/close in sequence against a real host.
close_reason: Completed: 5 integration tests in gate_s5_cli_to_host.rs verify full CLI-to-host round-trip with real HostRequestHandler — open/send/capture/list/inspect/close with text and JSON output formatting

title: Gate test for S5: UI client receives live session output
description: Verify that a UI client connected to the host receives live SessionOutput broadcasts when a session produces output. Start host, open workspace, attach UI client, send input to a session, and verify the UI client receives SessionOutput messages via the IPC push channel. Also verify SessionStateChanged notifications when a session exits. Expected outcome: UI clients see real-time terminal output without polling. Completion evidence: integration test verifies push messages arrive at UI client within expected latency.
close_reason: Completed: 4 integration tests in gate_s5_ui_session_output.rs verify UI client receives live SessionOutput broadcasts (marker text, state-changed on exit, latency under 2s, multi-client broadcast)

title: Gate test for S5: Action dispatch modifies workspace via IPC
description: Verify that actions dispatched via CLI (wtd action split-right, split-down, close-pane, etc.) modify the workspace layout via the real host request handler. Open a workspace, invoke split-right via IPC, verify the layout tree now has two panes. Invoke close-pane, verify the layout returns to one pane. Expected outcome: IPC-dispatched actions have real effects on workspace state. Completion evidence: integration test verifies layout tree changes after each action dispatch.
close_reason: Completed: 5 gate tests verify IPC-dispatched actions (split-right, split-down, close-pane, focus-next/prev-pane, error path) modify workspace layout state through the real HostRequestHandler

title: Investigate Shift+Enter mismatch in Codex CLI between WTD and Windows Terminal
description:
Observed in Codex CLI: Shift+Enter behavior under WTD does not match Windows Terminal. This likely affects multiline editing or submit-vs-newline behavior in the Codex input area.

Investigate:
- whether WTD live keyboard handling and key injection encode Shift+Enter differently from WT
- whether Codex sees a different key sequence in WTD than in WT
- whether ConPTY mode, bracketed paste, or keyboard enhancement negotiation changes the result

Comparison points:
- raw bytes produced for Enter vs Shift+Enter in WT and WTD
- Crossterm/terminal backend expectations for modified Enter on Windows
- whether Codex enables enhanced keyboard reporting and whether WTD forwards it correctly
close_reason: Investigated and root cause identified; follow-up fix tracked in wintermdriver-bbp

title: Reset local scrollback to live on pane input
description: Investigation wintermdriver-9wm found that WTD keeps per-pane scrollback offset in crates/wtd-ui/src/mouse_handler.rs and the paint path always renders using that offset, but the UI input paths in crates/wtd-ui/src/main.rs forward keyboard, paste, and VT mouse input with bridge.send_input(...) without calling MouseHandler::reset_scroll(). As a result the viewport stays scrolled back after the user starts typing. Follow-up work: reset scrollback offset, and likely clear stale selection, before forwarding live input to the active primary-screen pane.
close_reason: Reset local scrollback and stale selection on live pane input

title: Fix command palette shortcut handling and keybinding hints
description:
Command palette behavior is inconsistent with the active default bindings:

1. The UI toggles the palette via a hardcoded `Ctrl+Shift+Space` check in `crates/wtd-ui/src/main.rs`, but the default Windows Terminal preset binds `toggle-command-palette` to `Ctrl+Shift+P`.
2. The command palette entry list does not reliably show keyboard shortcuts for preset-provided bindings, because keybinding hints are built from raw `bindings.keys/chords` instead of the effective expanded bindings.

Observed impact:
- Users with default bindings cannot reliably open the palette using the documented command.
- The palette list may omit visible shortcut hints for commands.

Scope:
- Make palette toggle follow the effective bindings instead of a hardcoded chord.
- Ensure command palette entries display keybinding hints from expanded/effective bindings, including preset-only bindings.
- Add focused regression tests for both behaviors.
close_reason: Completed
